name: Vertical Egg Pressure Vessel
description: A four-story vertical pressure vessel shaped like an egg, the most pressure-efficient geometry for holding a breathable atmosphere above ground on Mars. A single 3D printer traces one continuous upward-spiraling wall of regolith-polymer composite, pausing at each opening so the arm can seat a prefabricated airlock and window before the print locks them permanently in place. The wall tapers into a sealed dome apex, with interior floor joists and deck panels dividing the volume into habitable levels.
engineering_rationale: Mars surface pressure is about 0.6 kPa, leaving roughly a 100 kPa differential against the pressurized interior. Flat or boxy walls would bow and crack under that load, so the habitat uses an ovoid pressure-vessel shell that carries the load in membrane tension. Continuous spiral printing avoids cold joints between layers, and embedding the prefab frames mid-print removes separate sealing and fastening steps.

structure.notes: Spiral-printed shell with prefab openings embedded during the print.

components.label: Foundation ring
components.description: Print the keyed circular base ring that anchors the pressure vessel to the prepared regolith pad.

components.label: Lower wall (to airlock height)
components.description: Spiral the composite wall upward to airlock height, leaving a door-sized opening in the print path.

components.label: Place airlock
components.description: Arm lifts the prefabricated composite airlock assembly and seats it into the door opening, flush with the surrounding print.

components.label: Mid wall (over airlock, to window height)
components.description: Resume printing directly over the airlock flange to lock it in, then continue the spiral to window height, leaving a window opening.

components.label: Place window
components.description: Arm seats the reinforced composite window frame into the window opening before the next layer prints over its edge.

components.label: Place floor joists
components.description: Arm installs the interior floor joists spanning the shell. Depends only on the mid wall, so it runs in parallel with the upper-wall and dome print.

components.label: Place deck panels
components.description: Arm lays deck panels across the installed joists to form the habitable floors.

components.label: Upper wall
components.description: Print over the window and complete the cylindrical wall up to the dome spring line.

components.label: Dome taper
components.description: Progressively taper the wall inward, corbeling the print into the egg-shaped dome.

components.label: Seal apex
components.description: Close the final aperture at the top of the dome into an airtight apex cap.
